murciélago.text: The one common Spanish word holding all five vowels — a bat.

cigüeña.text: The diéresis on ü makes it sound before e — a stork.

vergüenza.text: Diéresis again: g-u-e all pronounced — shame.

jirafa.text: The j spells the /x/ sound; g before i would sound the same — a giraffe.

almohada.text: From Arabic 'al-mihadda'; the h is silent — a pillow.

zanahoria.text: Silent h inside, z for the /s/ sound (seseo) — a carrot.

psicólogo.text: From Greek psykhe; the p is silent — a psychologist.

bilingüe.text: The diéresis on ü makes it heard — bilingual.

guitarra.text: gu keeps the hard g before i; the double r trills — a guitar.
